_comment: PAGENT kernel-mode entrypoint catalog for net/psmgr on Linux 6.3.5, scoped to WMI-3 findings. Advisory only: kernel-mode PoCs are freeform C programs run in the QEMU guest.

guest.exec_model: PoC is one userspace C program compiled into the guest rootfs and run as root in QEMU; KASAN/KMSAN/UBSAN or a matching kernel report is the crash signal.

entrypoints.driver: Create AF_PSMGR sockets. Creation reaches psmgr_create and allocates/initializes psmgr_session and psmgr_desc.
entrypoints.notes: Covers WMI-3 psmgr_create findings, including desc/session authoritative content.

entrypoints.interface: AF_PSMGR SOCK_SEQPACKET plus setsockopt(PSMGR_SOL, PSMGR_OPT_SET_ROLE/LOCAL_CTX) and sendmsg(2)
entrypoints.driver: Create a MANAGED AF_PSMGR socket, set role with PSMGR_OPT_SET_ROLE, install local context with PSMGR_OPT_LOCAL_CTX, then send crafted psmgr_msghdr payloads to drive psmgr_sendmsg -> psmgr_process_data.

entrypoints.interface: AF_PSMGR SOCK_SEQPACKET; primary/secondary or managed setup followed by sendmsg of PSMGR_MSG_COMMIT_REQ
entrypoints.driver: Establish a PSMGR session using setsockopt negotiation, then send a message with type PSMGR_MSG_COMMIT_REQ to drive psmgr_sendmsg -> psmgr_process_commit_req.
